nativeRange.habitat: Endemic to freshwater streams and seasonal pools in Madagascar; tubers tend to persist through dry-season dormancy.

decorPreferences: nutrient-rich substrate with root tabs around the bulb
fine gravel or sand with bulb placed on top or only half-buried (avoid fully burying)
open background area with ample vertical clearance for tall, ruffled strap leaves
soft to moderately hard, clean water with moderate flow

avoidWith: plant-eating fish such as silver dollars or buenos aires tetras

summary: Madagascar endemic bulb plant with long, ruffled olive-green strap leaves. Generally considered a background centerpiece for medium-to-large tanks.
careNotes: Aponogeton longiplumulosus is a Madagascar-endemic bulb plant known for its long, fluted or crinkled olive green strap leaves and slender lavender-to-dark violet flower spikes. Leaves have been reported to reach roughly 20-35 cm (with petioles to about 60 cm), and overall plant height in cultivation is commonly reported between 50 and 100 cm, which makes it generally advised as a background or focal specimen in tanks of about 150 L or larger with good vertical clearance. The bulb should be placed on top of the substrate or only half-buried — fully burying the bulb tends to cause rot. Temperatures around 23-28 °C (roughly 73-83 °F per Modern Aquarium), a pH near neutral (~6.5-7.5), and soft to moderately hard water are commonly recommended; it tends to do best in soft, clean water. Lighting in the medium range is typically sufficient, with a nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil or root tabs) giving the best growth. CO2 injection is optional but is often reported to improve leaf quality and growth rate. IMPORTANT: this species has a natural dormancy cycle — after a period of active growth (often following flowering) the leaves typically die back and the bulb rests for several weeks. Do NOT discard the bulb when this happens; leave it in the substrate (or store it in cool, damp conditions) and new leaves typically resprout. Note: the originally-designated Buce Plant primary URL returned 404 and was abandoned; Modern Aquarium was promoted to primary and Wikipedia served as the second confirming source.
breedingNotes: Generally propagated from seed produced on emergent flower spikes, though germination has been reported as difficult. Mature plants occasionally form daughter tubers that can be separated; tuber/bulb division is the more reliable propagation method in aquaria.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Originally-designated Buce Plant primary URL returned 404; promoted Modern Aquarium (designated secondary) to primary. Confirmed Madagascar origin, midground/background placement, medium growth, dormancy behaviour, temperature range 73-83 F, low-moderate lighting, leaf dimensions to ~61 cm long.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Aponogetonaceae, Alismatales), Madagascar endemism, leaf dimensions (20-35 cm long, ~6 cm wide, petiole ~60 cm), dark violet flowers, dormancy behaviour, medium-to-bright lighting preference, benefit from CO2 supplementation, seed propagation with difficult germination. Aquarium Co-Op product URL was tried as a third source but returned 404; Tropica and Aquasabi product pages were also tried but did not return useful data.
